Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs and opposing parties Wiley Utterback and Ellen Josephine Utterback.

RULING: SPECIALLY-APPEARING UNITED STATES FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT ASSOCIATED INSULATION OF CALIFORNIA
Parties stipulated to hear all matters simultaneously. Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Specially-Appearing United States Fire Insurance Company's Motion to Set Aside Default and Default Judgment Against Defendant Associated Insulation of California is denied. U.S. Fire lacks standing to bring this motion or appear in the action at this time. (W. Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal. App. 4th 1196, 1205 n. 12 ["Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 387, the trial court has discretion to permit a nonparty to intervene where the nonparty has a direct and immediate interest in the action"].) U.S. Fire has not demonstrated that it remains liable for any default judgment against Associated. (Id.) Moving party's request for Judicial Notice for Exhibits 2-11 is granted by the Court per Evidence Code 452(d). Court declines to take Judicial Notice of Exhibit 1. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Miranda Rumsey, CSR # 14199, csrmirandar@gmail.com (Tel: 530-613-9821) (503/CML)

The court will need a generic statement of the case from counsel on Tuesday morning. Counsel will not need to be present for jury hardshipping. The motion to consolidate Utterback will be served on defendants in Groups 1 and 2 as well as the Utterback defense counsel.

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Joanna Drozd, Esq. of COOLEY MANION JONES HAKE KUROWSKI LLP (415-512-4381), appearing for defendant / moving party Collins Electrical Company, Inc.
Lance R. Stewart, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Wiley Utterback, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT COLLINS ELECTRICAL COMPANY, INC.'S DEFENDANT COLLINS ELECTRICAL COMPANY, INC.S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Collins Electrical Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Utterback was exposed to asbestos-containing materials disturbed by defendant. Plaintiff's special interrogatory responses and deposition testimony preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's separate statement did not demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant owed a duty to Mr. Utterback. Even if defendant had satisfied its burden of production, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether Mr. Utterback was exposed to asbestos-containing fireproofing disturbed by defendant and Dr. Richard Cohen's declaration creates a triable issue whether defendant knew or should have known of the hazards of asbestos. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration sustained as to #2, 3, 4, 5, 7 and overruled as to #1, 6, 8, 9. Defendant's objections to Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled, except for objection 13 to the last sentence of paragraph 23, which is sustained. Order signed in open court. The Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
